title: Feature Representation & Deep Face Embeddings
concept: Deep face recognition maps facial images to low-dimensional vector spaces (embeddings) where distances represent face similarity.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main purpose of facial alignment in pre-processing?

options.text: To standardize rotation and scale so the face matches a fixed template.
To classify the emotion of the user.
To detect if the face is a printed photograph.

exercises.explain: Correct! Alignment rotates and scales tilted crops so the face is level, making it easier for recognition models to compare.

cards.title: Reducing a face to numbers
cards.body: If you want to compare two faces, you can't just compare their raw pixels. A slight change in lighting or position would make the pixels look completely different. Instead, neural networks reduce the entire face to a signature: a short list of numbers that capture the essence of a person's features.

cards.title: What is a Face Embedding?
cards.body: A face embedding is a low-dimensional vector (typically 128 or 512 numbers) extracted by a deep CNN or Vision Transformer. The model is trained so that photos of the same person produce very similar vectors, while photos of different people produce very different vectors.

cards.title: Coordinates on a map
cards.body: Think of a face embedding like GPS coordinates on a map. If two people are standing in the same room, their coordinates are very close. Similarly, two different photos of you will map to points that are very close to each other in this multi-dimensional 'face space'.

cards.title: FaceNet and ArcFace
cards.body: State-of-the-art models like FaceNet and ArcFace take an aligned face crop and output a 128D or 512D embedding vector. The software then uses simple math to compare these vectors, group photos of the same person, or verify identity.

cards.title: They don't store your photo
cards.body: You might think face recognition systems store your actual photos in their secure database. In reality, they discard the raw image and only store the embedding vector. This vector is one-way: a hacker cannot reconstruct your actual face image from the numbers.

cards.title: Pixels vs. Embeddings
cards.body: How face representations change in the pipeline:

left.text:
Millions of RGB values
Highly sensitive to lighting
Hard to compare directly

right.text:
512 decimal values
Robust to pose/lighting
Extremely fast to compare

exercises.prompt: What is a deep face embedding?

options.text: A low-dimensional vector representation of facial features
A high-resolution 3D scan of a person's face
An encryption key generated using standard web passwords
A pixel mask generated by instance segmentation

exercises.explain: Face embeddings are lists of numbers (vectors) representing key facial features in a compact form.

exercises.prompt: True or False: It is possible to easily reconstruct a high-quality photo of a user's face from their stored embedding vector.
exercises.explain: False. Embeddings are one-way feature extractions; they represent abstract traits, making image reconstruction extremely difficult.

exercises.sentence: Face embeddings are compact representations typically containing 128 or ___ numbers.
exercises.explain: Most modern deep face recognition models output embedding vectors of length 128 or 512.

exercises.prompt: Which of these outputs represents a face recognition model's embedding extraction?

options.text: Pixel Mask: 1s inside face contour, 0s outside

exercises.explain: A list of decimal numbers (a vector) is the output format of a face embedding model.

exercises.prompt: You are building a security scanner for an office. The database must check faces quickly without violating GDPR data retention rules (which restrict storing raw photos of employees). What should you do?

options.text: Extract and store only the face embedding vectors in the database, discarding the raw face photos immediately.
Store high-resolution JPEGs of all employees in a public folder.
Only use face detection, because finding where a face is does not identify individuals.
Save the images as encrypted ZIP files and decrypt them on every scan.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Storing embeddings instead of raw photos complies with privacy guidelines, keeps the system secure, and allows fast mathematical comparisons. Outstanding! Next: how to compare those embedding vectors.
